The actor doesn’t think Gary is a bad guy, explaining, “I think Gary did a bad thing. And I believe that Teri knows deep down that Gary is good. But does she sense that he’s troubled, possibly?”
Where Is Teri Collins Now and Is She Alive?
This is not the last time people will see Teri. There is still much more to her story, Okuma says.
“But this show will surprise you,” she says. “Just when you think you know what’s going to happen... you don’t know where it’s going to go. And that’s the fun of this show.”
She says that “anything and everything is possible” and even though Teri, as seen earlier this season, has a physical ailment, she “doesn’t let it stop her.”

“You see that right from the beginning that she’s on a mission, she has a plan with her life, and she doesn’t want to be derailed,” she says. “Then we see in Episode 5, she’s still carrying that pain, but she’s still leading and she’s still just chugging along. The pain is not going to stop her.”
So wherever Teri may be, she will keep fighting and people will discover more.
“We can expect more twists and turns that are going to take us to places that you didn’t think would happen,” Okuma says, noting the importance of the flashbacks in the storytelling of the show.

“It always informs the major plotline that is chugging along. So, we can expect more of that for sure. But rest assured, it’s always leading us to somewhere. It’s leading us to a destination, that in the end, it’s all going to make sense.”

title: Timothée Chalamet's Controversial Comments About Opera and Ballet, Explained
summary: Timothée Chalamet is finding out that people very much care about opera and ballet after his recent comments about both.
story: Timothée Chalamet is finding out that people very much care about opera and ballet after his recent comments about both.
The “Marty Supreme” star has faced a backlash after declaring that ballet and opera are arts that “no one cares about” during an interview in late February.
Chalamet’s remarks have triggered responses that he needs a night out at the opera to see what he’s missing and much less friendly replies.
Here’s what to know about the controversy, which bloomed just over a week before the 2026 Academy Awards, where Chalamet is a prime contender for best actor for “Marty Supreme.”
What Did Timothée Chalamet Say About Opera and Ballet?

Chalamet, 30, was speaking with fellow actor Matthew McConaughey on Feb. 21 at the CNN & Variety Town Hall Event when the conversation turned toward making films that will get more people to come out and see them in theaters.

“I’m really right in the middle, Matthew,” he said. “Because I admire people, and I’ve done it myself, (who) go on a talk show and go, ‘Hey, we got to keep movie theaters alive. You know, we got to keep this genre alive.’ And another part of me feels like, if people want to see it, like ‘Barbie,’ like ‘Oppenheimer,’ they’re going to go see it and go out of their way and be loud and proud about it.”

“And I don’t want to be working in ballet or opera, or things where it’s like, ‘Hey, keep this thing alive,’” he continued. “Even though it’s like, no one cares about this anymore. All respect to the ballet and opera people out there.”
Chalamet then appeared to realize how those comments might land in the opera and ballet worlds and joked, “I just lost 14 cents in viewership.”
How Did the Opera and Ballet Communities React?
A host of opera houses responded to Chalamet’s comments with everything from invites for the actor to come there to watch a performance to using his name as a promo code for a discount.
This one’s for you, @tchalamet… 👀” New York City’s Metropolitan Opera captioned an Instagram video of all the behind-the-scenes work that goes into a show.
The Royal Ballet Opera wrote on Instagram that “thousands” of people gather every night for ballet and opera and told Chalamet that if he’d “like to reconsider, our doors are open.”
The Seattle Opera wrote on Instagram that it was offering 14% off select seats to its performance of “Carmen” by using the promo code “TIMOTHEE.”
“Timmy, you’re welcome to use it too 💃 See you at the opera!” the organization wrote.
“@tchalamet, this is your official invitation. Let’s see if we can change your mind 👀” the English National Opera wrote on Instagram.
Other responses weren’t so benign.

“Honestly, I’m shocked that someone so seemingly successful can be so ineloquent and narrow minded in his views about art while considering himself as artist as I would only imagine one would as an actor,” Grammy-winning opera singer Isabel Leonard commented on a Variety Instagram video of Chalamet’s remarks.

“To take cheap shots at fellow artists says more in this interview than anything else he could say,” she continued. “Shows a lot about his character. You don’t have to like all art but only a weak person/artist feels the need to diminish in fact the VERY arts that would inspire those who are interested in slowing down, to do exactly that.”
New York City Ballet principal dancer Megan Fairchild also weighed in with an Instagram video.

“It’s not even the idea that he dissed ballet and opera that bothers me,” she said. “It’s the suggestion that he had the talent and aptitude to pursue these Olympic-level artistic fields in the first place. Timmy, I didn’t realize you were a world-class dancer or opera singer who simply chose not pursue it because acting’s more popular!”
A reference to the controversy also made its way into the “Weekend Update” segment on “Saturday Night Live” on March 7.

“Timothée Chalamet is being criticized by major opera and ballet organizations after he said that no one cares about those art forms,” host Colin Jost said. “Chalamet made the comment on a press tour for his movie about ping-pong.”

title: How to Watch All 10 Best Picture Nominees on Streaming Ahead of the 2026 Oscars
summary: Ahead of the 2026 Oscars on March 15, here's how to watch all 10 films nominated for best picture at home — “Bugonia,” F1,” “Frankenstein,” “Hamnet,” “Marty Supreme,” “One Battle After Another,” “The Secret Agent,” “Sentimental Value,” “Sinners” and “Train Dreams.”
story: Before filling out your own Oscar ballot, you can watch all 10 of the films nominated for the marquee award of the night right at home.
The 98th Academy Awards ceremony will be held on Sunday, March 15, at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles, which means there's still time to watch the movies up for best picture.
From modern Formula 1 racing to the Shakespearean era, this year's best picture nominees span time and geography. Nearly all of the films are over two hours long, so make sure to get comfortable.
Last year, "Anora" won best picture as well as awards for best actress, best director, best film editing and best original screenplay.
Read on for how to watch all 10 of the movies up for the 2026 award.
'Bugonia'

This film stars Emma Stone as a successful and powerful CEO kidnapped by two conspiracy theorist-obsessed young men who are convinced she's an alien intent on destroying the Earth. The film is nominated in the categories of best actress (for Stone), adapted screenplay and original score.

"F1" stars Brad Pitt as a retired car racing legend persuaded into leading a struggling Formula 1 driving team. The film was nominated for four Oscars: best picture, film editing, sound and visual effects.
'Frankenstein'

An adaptation of the classic novel of the same name by Mary Shelley, this movie was directed by Guillermo del Toro of "The Shape of Water" and "Pan's Labyrinth." The film stars Oscar Isaac as Frankenstein and Jacob Elordi as the monster. "Frankenstein" was nominated for nine awards total at this year's Oscars: best picture, best supporting actor (for Elordi), adapted screenplay, cinematography, costume design, sound, original score, makeup and hairstyling, and production design.
'Hamnet'

A tale set in the 16th century, "Hamnet" is based on the 2020 novel of the same name by Maggie O'Farrell. The film stars Paul Mescal and Jessie Buckley as Shakespeare and his wife, Agnes, in a fictional retelling of how the couple lost their son, Hamnet, and how it inspired Shakespeare's classic "Hamlet." The film is nominated in the categories of best picture, best actress (for Buckley), directing, adapted screenplay, casting, costume design, original score and production design.
'Marty Supreme'

Timotheé Chalamet stars as Marty Mauser (based on real-life athlete Marty Reisman) in this comedic sports drama about an accomplished table tennis star who goes to extreme lengths to attend the world championship in Japan. The movie was nominated for nine Oscars: best picture, best actor (for Chalamet), directing, original screenplay, casting, cinematography, costume design, production design and film editing.
'One Battle After Another'

Starring Leonardo DiCaprio as Pat Calhoun, this film follows DiCaprio as a former revolutionary who thought he had left the past behind him, until an old rival makes a shocking reappearance. The movie was nominated for an impressive thirteen Oscars: best picture, best actor (for DiCaprio), best supporting actress (for Teyana Taylor), best supporting actor (for Benicio Del Toro and Sean Penn) directing, adapted screenplay, casting, cinematography, sound, original score, production design and film editing.
'The Secret Agent'

This Brazilian film is primarily in Portuguese and was nominated for four Oscars, including best actor (for Wagner Moura), casting and international feature film. The story follows Armando Solimões (Moura), a technology expert fleeing from a mysterious past. Solimões ends up back in his hometown of Recife, caught in turmoil during Brazil's military dictatorship in the 1970s.
'Sentimental Value'

"Sentimental Value" follows a family attempting to reunite, detailing an emotional and rich history across generations. The film stars Renate Reinsve, Stellan Skarsgård, Inga Ibsdotter Lilleaas and Elle Fanning. The film is primarily in Norwegian and received a total of nine Oscar nominations: best picture, best actress (for Reinsve), best supporting actress (for Fanning and Lilleaas), best supporting actor (for Skarsgard), directing, original screenplay, international feature film and film editing.
'Sinners'

This action/horror movie stars Michael B. Jordan in two roles, Smoke and Stack, rebellious twins in the Jim Crow era South. On the night they are set to open up their own juke joint, disturbing events start to occur around them.

"Sinners" was nominated for 16 total Oscar nominations, the most ever for a single film: best picture, actor in a leading role (for Jordan), best supporting actress (for Wunmi Mosaku), best supporting actor (for Delroy Lindo), directing, original screenplay, casting, cinematography, costume design, sound, original score, original song, makeup and hairstyling, production design, film editing and visual effects.
'Train Dreams'

Based on a 2011 novella of the same name by Denis Johnson, "Train Dreams" stars Joel Edgerton as an American logger and railroad worker in the early 20th century. Edgerton's character marries Gladys (Felicity Jones), and the film follows the family throughout a few years of their life. "Train Dreams" was also nominated for adapted screenplay, cinematography and original song.

title: When and Where to Watch the Latest ‘Bachelor Mansion Takeover’ Episode
summary: Members of Bachelor Nation are swapping roses for power tools to compete on the new renovation series “Bachelor Mansion Takeover.” Find out how to tune in.
story: Members of Bachelor Nation are swapping roses for power tools to compete on the new HGTV renovation series “Bachelor Mansion Takeover.”

Alums from “The Bachelor,” “The Bachelorette” and its “Golden” spinoffs who have backgrounds in interior design, real estate and carpentry are putting their skills to the test and seeing if they can update the iconic Bachelor Mansion.

The property, located in Agoura Hills, California, has been the starting point of nearly every season since 2007. There, the Bachelor or Bachelorette meets their suitors for the first time as they arrive in limousines. The first rose ceremony of the season is held later that night, and the contestants who receive a rose move in usually the next day.

But after being the temporary home for contestants for nearly 20 years, the home is in need of a major upgrade. So, 12 past contestants move into the mansion and renovate it room by room in each episode of “Bachelor Mansion Takeover,” which premiered March 2.

Hosted by Jesse Palmer, each episode shows the reality television stars compete in challenges and work together to impress judges, and fellow alums, Tyler Cameron and Tayshia Adams. Contestants are eliminated during the episodes until the last one standing wins the $100,000 prize.
In addition to Cameron and Adams, other special guests will appear every episode to help judge the final designs.
Find out how to catch up and when to tune in, below. Plus, get to know the cast, where you’ve seen them before and (*spoiler warning*) who’s already been eliminated.
What Day Does ‘Bachelor Mansion Takeover’ Air?
New episodes of “Bachelor Mansion Takeover” arrive weekly on Mondays at 8 p.m. ET/PT.
Where to Watch and Stream ‘Bachelor Mansion Takeover’
“Bachelor Mansion Takeover” airs on HGTV. The episodes are also available to stream the following day on HBO Max and discovery+.
Meet the ‘Bachelor Mansion Takeover’ Cast
These 12 Bachelor Nation stars are showing off their interior design and carpentry skills on the new HGTV renovation series. Every episode, people are eliminated.

title: Alysa Liu Says People Have Been Mispronouncing Her Name. She Explains How to Say It
summary: Anyone who watched the Milan Cortina Winter Olympics knows Alysa Liu’s name.
story: Anyone who watched the Milan Cortina Winter Olympics knows Alysa Liu’s name.
But do you know how to pronounce it correctly?

The figure skating star and Olympic gold medalist gave a primer on how to properly say her name in a video interview with Newsweek. It turns out that broadcasters and fans alike were getting it wrong while marveling at the 20-year-old becoming the first U.S. women’s figure skater in 24 years to win Olympic gold.
“So technically this is how my family says my name: Ah-LEE-sah Lee-oh,” Liu says in the video.
She also knows that not everyone is going to get it right — even her friends.

“That’s technically how you say my name, but ‘Ah-LISS-uh’ is how my friends call me, and that’s how the world calls me, and then my last name a lot of people say ‘Lee-oo,’ ‘Loo,’” she said. “I personally don’t care because technically the right way is ‘Lee-oh,’ but that can be hard to pronounce.
“I don’t expect people to know how to say that.”
No matter how you say it, Liu was one of the faces of the 2026 Winter Olympics with her signature style, joyous skating, approach to competition and unorthodox upbringing.

She also became a poster girl for recovering from burnout to find joy again in something she loves. Liu became the youngest U.S. women’s national champion in history when she was only 13, quit the sport at 16 and then made a comeback that culminated in her gold medal in Italy.

During an appearance on TODAY on March 1, Liu shared advice for others who may be struggling with their mental health as well as tips for parents pushing their children to continue to compete in a sport that they want to step back from.

“My mental health used to be horrible, like, absolutely horrible,” Liu told TODAY.com. “I just took a break, and I did something new. I moved on to a different part of my life. I really focused on school and friendship, and honestly, that’s the thing that grounded me.”

title: All About Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy and John F. Kennedy Jr.'s wedding location, photos, documentary, date and pictures.
summary: What happened during JFK Jr and Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy's wedding? Here are details about the wedding including the location.
story: "Love Story" follows Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy and John F. Kennedy Jr. as their private love story blossoms into a public spectacle.

For their wedding, the characters — played by Sarah Pidgeon and Paul Anthony Kelly — try to carve out a space just for their, well, love story. Bessette and Kennedy's real wedding was as secret as the show makes it seem to be.
In 2019, TLC aired never-before-seen footage of the secret 1996 wedding in a special titled "JFK And Carolyn’s Wedding: The Lost Tapes."
Here's what we know about the real 1996 ceremony, which unfolded in a small island in Georgia.
The Guest List Was Kept Intentionally Small

In the book "Once Upon a Time: The Captivating Life of Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy," author Elizabeth Beller explains that there were only around 40 guests at the wedding since the bride and groom wanted to keep the list small.
"How well Carolyn and John felt someone could keep a secret determined the timing of when they got the call to clear the weekend of September 21," the book reads.

For instance, Kennedy's best man Anthony Radziwill learned about the nuptials several weeks before they take place. Christiane Amanpour, one of Kennedy's roommates while they both attended college, also learned weeks ahead of time so she could fly back to the U.S. from Sarajevo, where she was on assignment for CNN.
The Wedding Took Place on a Remote Georgia Island
Cumberland Island is Georgia’s southernmost barrier island. Its 17 miles consist of coastline, maritime forests and fields where wild horses roam.
According to Beller's book, Kennedy first visited with an ex-girlfriend, Christina Haag.
Cumberland Island has a daily visitor limit of 300 people, according to the National Park Service, although that number may be increased thanks to a new management plan.
Accessible only by private boat, ferry or helicopter, its remoteness was part of the appeal.
The Couple’s Guests Didn’t Know The Exact Location of the Wedding
In “JFK And Carolyn’s Wedding: The Lost Tapes,” Kennedy’s close friend Billy Noonan describes the experience of traveling to the wedding.
“We knew there was a wedding but we didn’t know when or where. It was almost like they were eloping,” he said.
Once guests arrived in Florida, they drove to the Georgia state line then took a ferry from St. Marys to Cumberland Island, a small island off the coast of Georgia.
Kennedy's Friend Filmed the Wedding
Noonan filmed the wedding festivities on a handheld camcorder Kennedy had given him for his own bachelor party.

“It sort of became a talisman or a mascot documenting these milestones in our friends’ lives. So he said to me, ‘You still have that videotape we gave you?’ And I said, ‘Yeah.’ And he said, ‘Well bring it down.’ So I brought it down there not knowing what I’m doing,” Noonan said in the documentary.

“If I were a videographer, it never would have looked like that. It was haphazard, it’s light, it’s dark, the audio is not right. But it plays into the whole thing of the weekend was that nothing was scripted.”
Denis Reggie, the brother of Sen. Ted Kennedy’s wife, was the only photographer allowed at the ceremony.
How Kennedy Chose His Wedding Party
In “JFK And Carolyn’s Wedding: The Lost Tapes,” The Knot editor Esther Lee describes how Kennedy selected his side of the wedding party.
“It was an incredibly thoughtful process for JFK Jr. and Caroline. JFK Jr. ended up picking a cousin from each faction of the family,” she said.
And Yes, Caroline Kennedy Was the Maid of Honor
Kennedy’s sister, Caroline Kennedy Schlossberg, also played an important role in the festivities. "Love Story"
Per the Los Angeles Times, she was Bessette’s maid of honor.
Caroline Bessette Considered Jacqueline Kennedy While Planning the Wedding

In the book “Once Upon a Time: The Captivating Life of Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy,” Beller explains how Caroline Bessette kept the memory of Jacqueline Kennedy, who died in 1994, while planning her wedding.

“Carolyn spent weeks discussing flowers with Bunny Mellon, which served as a lesson in landscape architecture, horticulture, decorating, and, saving the best for last, an intensive study in ‘How Jackie Would Do It.’ It mattered to Carolyn, who had never met Jackie, that the wedding would have met her late mother-in-law’s approval,” the book reads.
Ted Kennedy Gave a Toast at the Rehearsal Dinner
Kennedy lost his father, John F. Kennedy, just days before he turned 3 years old. His mother died in 1994 of cancer.

At his wedding rehearsal dinner, the groom’s uncle, Ted Kennedy, served as a parental figure and gave a toast, which can be seen, in part, in the documentary “JFK And Carolyn’s Wedding: The Lost Tapes.”

While addressing the Bessette family, the senator said, “We want to just say that you’re not only gaining John as a son-in-law, but you’re gaining 75 Kennedys. We promise that we will only visit you five to 10 times a year. And not until after the honeymoon.”
Chermayeff, who was in attendance at the wedding, reacts to the moment in the documentary as she watches the footage.
“I loved that speech. Teddy was John’s closest father figure, really,” she said.
The Couple Got Married at the First African Baptist Church

The location of the wedding ceremony, the First African Baptist Church, was a “nod to both JKF’s and RJK’s dedication to civil rights,” per “Once Upon a Time: The Captivating Life of Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy.”

“The one-room church was built in 1937, replacing the original log cabin from 1893; some of the founders had been born into slavery and emancipated after the Civil War. Carolyn and John had planned a Catholic service, and John said they had ‘turned Catholicism on its head’ in order for Father Charles O’Byrne, the Jesuit priest who had conducted Jackie’s funeral, to conduct the ceremony in a Baptist church,” the book reads.
Helicopters Tried to Catch a Glimpse of the Wedding
Despite their best efforts to keep the wedding private, the couple’s special day was almost ruined by helicopters circling overhead.
“They were hovering and we were all like, ‘Oh God,’” Chermayeff recalls in “JFK And Carolyn’s Wedding: The Lost Tapes.”
“When we first saw those helicopters we knew that we were going to have a problem,” Noonan recalls in the documentary.
To fend off the helicopters, guests hid by nearby trees and eventually lost them.
The Ceremony Started 2 Hours Late
The wedding was supposed to begin at 5 p.m., per “Once Upon a Time: The Captivating Life of Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy,” but a few wardrobe mishaps delayed the start time.

"John had misplaced his shirt, and Carolyn tried to put her dress on in the car on the way to the chapel. She forgot that she needed to put the dress on before attending to her hair and makeup, which meant redoing her hair and makeup after she had pulled the form-fitting work of art over hear head," the book reads.
When John showed up to the chapel, he was "relieved that he was not later" than his bride.
Since there was no electricity in the chapel, guests were concerned that it was getting later. However, they improvised and decided to have a candle-lit ceremony.
"And that gave a very sacred, quiet feel to it and it was so beautiful," Chermayeff recalls in “JFK And Carolyn’s Wedding: The Lost Tapes.”
The Couple Asked a Gospel Singer to Sing at Their Wedding
The bride and groom asked gospel singer David R. Davis to perform at their ceremony and had one song request in mind: "Amazing Grace."
"(The groom) wanted, as it were, spirituals. 'Amazing Grace' is a hymn and a spiritual," he said in “JFK And Carolyn’s Wedding: The Lost Tapes.”
At first, Davis wasn't interested in singing at the wedding, but Senator Kennedy convinced him.

“He had heard how I tried to get out of doing this wedding. He said, ‘I know you think you’re not supposed to be here but we wanted somebody here that knows about the things of God.’ And I knew by the divine providence of God is the reason that I was there. So for the first time, I was consciously glad that I did not win the fight to not do the wedding,” he said.
Bessette Asked Narciso Rodriguez to Design Her Wedding Dress
While working at Calvin Klein, Bessette became friends with Narciso Rodriguez, who is now a popular designer. She asked Rodriguez to design her wedding gown.

“His dress was iconic and it was brilliant and it was beautiful. But at the time it was completely different from any other wedding dress during the ‘90s that we had seen. During that decade, we saw a lot of ball gowns and princess-y type of pieces. And yet here’s Carolyn Bessette. She’s essentially becoming America’s princess and she doesn’t choose anything out of a fairy tale movie. Instead, she chose something that was elegant and structured,” Lee said during “JFK And Carolyn’s Wedding: The Lost Tapes.”
The sleek slip dress had a cowl neckline and an open back. She paired the look with tulle gloves and a matching veil.
The Wedding Menu Was Simple and Elegant

Jodee Sadowsky, a sous chef at the wedding, appears in “JFK And Carolyn’s Wedding: The Lost Tapes” and describes the couple's wedding menu. It consisted of the following items: free range capons, poached artichokes, patty pan squash and Irish potatoes.
"It was a challenge but we got it done and and you know at the end of the whole thing it turned out very nice," Sadowsky said.

title: James Van Der Beek's 9-Year-Old Daughter Shares What She's Learned About Grief Since His Death
summary: James Van Der Beek’s 9-year-old daughter is reflecting on her father’s passing and sharing poignant advice for anyone else who is mourning the loss of a loved one.
story: James Van Der Beek’s 9-year-old daughter is reflecting on her father’s passing and sharing poignant advice for anyone else who is mourning the loss of a loved one.
On March 8, the actor’s wife Kimberly Van Der Beek shared a video that her daughter Emilia Van Der Beek filmed in honor of what would have been her father’s 49th birthday.
“Emilia asked me if she could make a video to post today, walked outside and came back with this,” the mother of six wrote.
Kimberly Van Der Beek also shared a message for her husband, writing, “James... We will celebrate March 8th every day for the rest of our lives.”
James Van Der Beek, who was diagnosed with colorectal cancer in 2023, died on Feb. 11.
In her video, Emilia explained her intention for addressing her father’s fans.
“I’m just going to tell you little tips or anything that helps you getting through anybody you love who’s passing,” she said.
Emilia proceeded to outline the following pieces of advice for anyone who is missing someone who passed away.
Do Still Believe in Miracles
The 9-year-old remembered a few words of wisdom that her father shared with her before his passing.

“Something my dad told me was, if this doesn’t work out the way he wanted it to and the way we wanted it to for him living, I still have to believe in miracles. Miracles can still happen, just later on in life. And they’ll keep coming,” she remembered.
“Just remember they have some work to do on the other side,” she said of people’s departed loved ones.
Do Let Your Emotions Out
Emilia called this the “number one thing” she has learned since her father passed away nearly a month ago.

“If you miss them, you can cry. You can talk to them. I talk to my dad every day and I start with a sentence and I say, ‘Hi dad, I miss you and I love you so much and I’ll never stop loving you.’ And I just tell him about my day, how I’m feeling. And I tell my family how I’m feeling. I know he can hear me, but I can’t hear him,” she said.
Emilia said your loved ones are “watching over you.”
“They’re a part of your body and in a good place,” she said.
The 9-year-old expressed her gratitude that her father no longer has to battle cancer.
“He’s not in pain anymore. He’s in heaven, above the clouds with God and the Lord,” she said.
Don’t Blame Yourself
Emilia underscored that it’s OK to cry when you need to, but warned people not to blame themselves after they lose a loved one.

“Cry, cry, cry. Be happy they’re in a good place. Be sad because you miss them. You can be angry. But don’t blame yourself. Blaming yourself, it’s not a good strategy for someone’s passing,” she said.
Don’t Compare Your Feelings to Others’
“They don’t know how you feel. Everybody has different emotions in their body and they express it in different ways, many, many different ways,” she said.
Emilia recommended going into the grieving process with full awareness that it’s “going to be hard.”
Do Hold Onto Mementos
Emilia, who was wearing a baseball hat during the video, revealed that it was one of her father’s hats.
“Something that I have done with my dad’s passing is I stole his hat. Steal anything of theirs and just keep it. My dad’s hat smells like him and I love it so much,” she said.
Do Keep Your Loved One’s Memory Alive
At the end of her video, Emilia shared a way to keep your loved one’s memory alive after their passing.
“I praise my dad. I know that he was a good man. A lot of people loved him and people prayed for him and he was loved by many, many hearts and many, many people,” she said.

title: Ryan Gosling Can’t Stop Breaking Character While Hosting ‘SNL’ for a 4th Time
summary: Ryan Gosling broke character and laughed during a few sketches while hosting “Saturday Night Live” for a fourth time.
story: Ryan Gosling had a serious case of the giggles — again — while hosting “Saturday Night Live” on March 7.
The three-time Oscar nominee broke character in multiple sketches during his fourth go-round hosting the long-running late-night show.

In his first sketch after the monologue, called “Wedding Tradition,” Gosling played a wedding guest who continued to clink his glass in order to make the bride and groom kiss while they give a speech.
“I’m clinking the bottom of the glass. When I clink the top of the glass, you kiss on the lips. So when I —,” he said before pausing to hold in a laugh.
“Guess what body part I want you to kiss now?” he said, as he guffawed again.
Gosling tried to stifle more laughter when the bride’s siblings came up to give a toast, prompting him to clink his glass again.
“I’m doing the siblings now,” he said as he held it together. “Kiss.”

Then in “Passing Notes,” the “Project Hail Mary” star played Mr. Witt, a principal visiting a class to discuss bullying. Things spiraled out of control, though, when the teacher (Ashley Padilla) kept spotting students passing notes to each other, which she and Mr. Witt read aloud.

“The contents of these notes have been changed since rehearsal,” read a message onscreen before Padilla recited the first note. Padilla began laughing when the first note referenced her character trying to get makeup tips from ChatGPT, while Gosling smiled and did his best to keep it together.
Gosling then grabbed a note from Marcello Hernandez, which he also read aloud.

“Don’t tell anyone this, but I came to school early the other day and Principal Witt was in the school gym shooting hoops. Oh, God, I saw him miss 133 layups in a row. After each miss, he said, ‘There’s got to be a way to do this,’” he read while chuckling. “When he finally made one, he tried to celebrate by ripping his T-shirt in half, but he couldn’t do it and just stretched it out really bad.”
Padilla and Gosling again struggled to keep it together when she read a third note about her going to see her gynecologist. She laughed and Gosling put his hand on his face to maintain his composure.
Gosling grabbed a fourth note, which played up his role as Ken in “Barbie”.

“This happened a few years ago. I went to see ‘Barbie’ with my sister and Principal Witt was there. He bought an entire row for himself. Every time the Ken character came on screen he’d say, “Finally, something to look at. If I was still gay, I’d do him,’” he said while trying not to laugh.

Gosling also broke character in “Cyclops,” in which he played one of three clueless cyclopes, along with Kenan Thompson and Mikey Day, tasked with solving a riddle from a pair of muses (Padilla and Veronika Slowikowska) to open a door to treasure.

Padilla laughed throughout the sketch as she begged the cyclopes to figure out the riddle and stop approaching the door. Gosling at one point used a prop staff to cover his face and hide his own giggles. Padilla, meanwhile, cackled again when he tried to rush the door.
“Please, my lord. Please, God, stop. I’m begging you, please” she said, while she and Slowikowska howled.

Gosling is no stranger to breaking character on “SNL." Most notably, he appeared in the infamous 2024 sketch "Beavis and Butt-Head" that had Heidi Gardner in stitches. In the skit, Gosling played Beavis and Mikey Day played Butt-Head during a town hall conversation about AI.
In 2015, during his first stint hosting, he also broke out in hysterics in the sketch "Close Encounters," where he played a man being interviewed after being abducted by aliens.

Gosling has acknowledged his propensity to laugh when hosting the show and compared himself to Jimmy Fallon, who was notorious for breaking character when he was a cast member on the show from 1998 to 2004.

“I get in trouble a lot for laughing on the show,” Gosling told Fallon on “The Tonight Show” on March 5. “People always bring you into it. So they always go, ‘You know, you laugh too much.’ You know? ‘You and Fallon!’”

title: The Advice Steve Carell Gives His Grown Kids — and What He Keeps To Himself
summary: Steve Carell talks about playing a father on the new HBO show "Rooster" and reveals the "balancing act" of parenting his two children now that they're adults.
story: You might not know it, but Steve Carell is shy.

It’s surprising given that he’s spent nearly four decades in front of the camera, appearing in classic TV shows and movies including “Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy,” “The Big Short,” “The 40-Year-Old Virgin,” “Crazy, Stupid, Love” and, of course, “The Office.”

His latest role in the HBO show “Rooster” finds him portraying Greg Russo, a well-meaning but occasionally cringy bestselling author who relocates to a Vermont college town after his adult daughter, Katie (Charly Clive), suffers a personal setback.
According to Carell, he sees a “fair percentage” of himself in his on-screen counterpart.
“I think he’s someone who doesn’t necessarily feel at ease in his professional world,” Carell tells TODAY.com in an interview.
“He inadvertently became a fairly famous writer and he feels a little awkward about it,” he says of the role.

“And I think I sort of have always felt that way about acting. It didn’t really suit my personality because I think I tend to be a little more shy. I related to that. You kind of have to push out of yourself and embrace it.”

Much of “Rooster” centers around Greg’s close, but somewhat complicated, relationship with Katie, a college professor who’s struggling to come to terms with the recent breakup of her marriage, as well as Greg’s acceptance of his own divorce five years earlier.
Also starring Phil Dunster, Annie Mumolo, Maximo Salas, Danielle Deadwyler and John C. McGinley, the result is a charming, funny and sometimes-serious look at life and all its ups and downs.

Think “Shrinking” meets “Ted Lasso” or “Scrubs” if it were set on a college campus. Which actually makes perfect sense given that “Rooster” is a product of Bill Lawrence and Matt Tarses, the creators behind the award-winning shows.
Carell, who’s an executive producer on “Rooster,” says it was important to him to ensure that Greg was a three-dimensional person and not just the punchline of a joke.

“I didn’t want the character to be a buffoon. I wanted him to be someone who’s funny and adept and smart. In the show, this really incredible, intelligent, powerful woman marries him,” says Carell of Greg’s ex-wife, Elizabeth, played by Connie Britton.

“So I figured there has to be something about him personally that is appealing, that is strong and attractive. But at the same time, I think he feels a little bit awkward at times and doesn’t always put the right foot forward.”

Though Greg may not always get it right, his willingness to do anything for his daughter, Katie, is more than apparent, not unlike Carell, who shares children Elisabeth Anne, 25, and John, 22, with long-time wife and SNL alum, Nancy Walls Carell.

“It’s a cliche, but you love your kids and you would do anything for your kids. But, at a certain point, you have to let them make their own mistakes. They’re not children anymore and you have to respect the fact that they’re adults and they have their own wants and needs and their own directions that they choose to pursue,” explains Carell.
“So, that’s a learning curve,” he sums up.

The same holds true for offering advice. As his on-screen counterpart learns, parent-child relationships can be tricky once kids become adults, and knowing when to step in and when to stand down isn’t always easy.

“I have a really good relationship with my daughter and I feel like there’s an inherent, mutual respect between us. I think she does respect my opinion, but I can’t always offer it. Sometimes I do, and most of the time I wait for her to ask,” Carell says.

“It’s a hard balancing act to be their dad while, at the same time, allow them to figure it out themselves because those battles that they have by themselves are the ones that are really going to give them strength and teach them something. It can’t all be handed to them.”

As for his new HBO show, Carell says that each successive episode  gets “better and better” and that much like Lawrence’s other hit shows, “Shrinking” and “Ted Lasso,” “Rooster” is funny, while still having “that common thread of human emotion that people can relate to.”
“Those are the shows I love to watch. I love to watch a show that’ll make me laugh, but actually care about who I’m watching, care about their relationships and their dynamics.”

title: ‘The Voice’ Season 29 Brings Back Music Legends As Advisors and Mega Mentors
summary: A slew of familiar faces are joining Kelly Clarkson, Adam Levine and John Legend for the next stage of “The Voice: Battle of Champions.”Season 29 of the NBC singing competition show is already underway, with just one more episode remaining of the Blind Auditions.
story: A slew of familiar faces are joining Kelly Clarkson, Adam Levine and John Legend for the next stage of “The Voice: Battle of Champions.”

Season 29 of the NBC singing competition show is already underway, with just one more episode remaining of the Blind Auditions. Then, those selected to compete on Team Kelly, Team Adam and Team Legend will move onto round two: The Battles.

In the teaser that will run at the end of the March 9 episode, fans will see what they can expect the rest of the season, including which special guests and members of “The Voice” family are returning for the upcoming rounds as advisors.

Ahead of the episode, TODAY exclusively revealed that for The Battles, Good Charlotte’s Benji Madden is teaming up with Levine, R&B singer Muni Long is helping Legend, and Jennifer Hudson, who was a coach on Seasons 13 and 15, will reunite with Clarkson.

After that, The Knockouts begin. TODAY also exclusively confirmed that two-time “Voice” champion Michael Bublé will work with Team Kelly as a mega mentor. Meanwhile, original “Voice” coach CeeLo Green, who was on the first three seasons of the show and Season 5, will serve as the mentor for Team Adam and Team Legend.

But this Knockouts round will have a new twist called the “In-Season All Star Competition.” Here’s how it works: Each coach will bring back two fan-favorite musicians who competed on their teams in previous seasons. The alums will rejoin their former teams and go head-to-head against each other in sing-offs. The coach who has the most sing-off wins will earn a second finalist in the finale.
Green will be featured again in this stage of the competition as he will decide the winner of each “All-Star Showdown” in the special episode.
So, who are the past artists that are stepping onto “The Voice” stage again? These are the returning singers:
Season 1 winner Javier ColonSeason 9 winner Jordan Smith
Season 1 winner Javier Colon
Season 9 winner Jordan Smith
Season 17 winner Jake HootSeason 21 winner Girl Named Tom
Season 17 winner Jake Hoot
Season 21 winner Girl Named Tom
Season 16 winner Maelyn JarmonSeason 27 fan-favorite Renzo
Season 16 winner Maelyn Jarmon
Season 27 fan-favorite Renzo
The Popstart teaser reveals more exciting elements, like the Super Steal, as well.
“Every decision matters more,” is said in a voiceover. “Expect the unexpected.”
Plus, Green’s grand return is so dramatic that Clarkson says she plans to “steal it” for her Las Vegas residency.
Make sure you don’t miss any of the wild moments and intense competition. The next episode of “The Voice: Battle of Champions” airs on Monday, March 9 at 9 p.m. ET/PT on NBC.

title: Charlie Puth Says This Taylor Swift Lyric Inspired His New Album — and Made Him Want to ‘Open Up’
summary: Charlie Puth says that Taylor Swift mentioning his name in her song "The Tortured Poets Department" inspired his new album. "Whatever's Clever."
story: Charlie Puth is crediting Taylor Swift for helping him grow.

Puth, whose album “Whatever’s Clever" comes out March 27, says the “Opalite” singer inspired him after she dropped his name in the 2024 song “The Tortured Poets Department” from her album of the same name.
“We declared Charlie Puth should be a bigger artist,” she sang.
“That’s the lyric that inspired this album,” Puth told Willie Geist in a preview clip of the upcoming March 8 edition of Sunday Sitdown.
“Bigger artist means that you have to open up," he added.
The “One Call Away” singer says Swift’s abilities as a singer and songwriter help her connect with others. He took her name-checking him as a reminder for him to evolve.
“That’s why millions of people can relate to her,” he said. “And I always felt like I kind of put a cap on how many people could relate to me. And that’s a scary thing to open that can of worms."
"You don’t hear that as, like, 'He’s so talented, he should be even more popular than he is.' You thought, she wants you to be a different kind of artist," Geist clarified.
Puth agreed and said his takeaway from that lyric could benefit other artists, as well.
“Yeah, and it would be advice that I would pass down to other artists, too,” he said. “It’s a good push to hear that in a song.”
Puth has spoken about being mentioned in “The Tortured Poets Department” before.
“I appreciate the compliment,” he said on TODAY with Hoda & Jenna in 2024. “I take it as a compliment.”
He called Swift’s shoutout “inspiring” and said it gave him pause about his own approach to songwriting.

“It made me think about different ways to write songs and her way of writing, being almost overly-detailed, in the music. I’m like, ‘I wonder if I can do a little bit of that, too,’” he said before adding at the time that he’s “working on it.”

title: Scott Patterson Sets Record Straight on His ‘Sullivan’s Crossing’ Exit
summary: Scott Patterson is sharing the real reason he decided not to return to "Sullivan's Crossing" for Season 4 of the hit series.
story: Scott Patterson is sharing the real reason he decided not to return to "Sullivan's Crossing" for Season 4 of the hit series.
For the past three seasons, the actor has played the role of Harry “Sully” Sullivan in the Canadian drama, which is based on the bestselling book series by Robyn Carr, who also wrote “Virgin River."
On March 4, Entertainment Weekly revealed that Patterson would not be returning for Season 4 of "Sullivan's Crossing."

"Season 3 of 'Sullivan’s Crossing' ended with Sully leaving for Ireland, beginning a new chapter in his life. Season 4 picks up the next day, with Sully still overseas," the show’s showrunner and executive producer Roma Roth said in a statement that the CW provided. "While he isn’t physically present in this season, the character remains an important part of the world with the potential to be included in future seasons should that align with the ongoing creative."
In response, Patterson shared his own side of the story in a statement on Instagram, attributing his decision to leave the show to "creative differences" that "were becoming untenable."

"It’s unfortunate that it is now being implied that they moved on from me/Sully when the fact is the complete opposite, and those who sadly already have spoken out are also fully aware of this fact, and yet chose to say otherwise," he added.
"Sullivan's Crossing" debuted in Canada in 2023 before premiering in the U.S. on the CW in October 2023. It is now available to stream on Netflix.
Patterson previously spoke to TODAY.com about his character, who runs a remote campground called Sullivan’s Crossing in a small town.

“He’s got a heart of gold. He’s always lending a helping hand where needed,” Patterson said. “He’s a man of few words, but he connects with people across a broad spectrum of the town that he lives in. So he’s kind of the unofficial mayor of that place.”
Season 4 of "Sullivan's Crossing" will premiere April 20. Read on for Patterson's full statement explaining his exit from the show.
Read Scott Patterson's Full Statement on His Exit from 'Sullivan's Crossing'
Every actor knows what it’s like to fall in love with a character and a story. I fell in love with Sully and have nothing but fondness for him.
The creative differences were becoming untenable and I just sadly realized that the show was not something that I could agree to continue.

It’s unfortunate that it is now being implied that they moved on from me/Sully when the fact is the complete opposite, and those who sadly already have spoken out are also fully aware of this fact, and yet chose to say otherwise.

I was not intending to make any statement but the fans of the books and the show deserve to know the truth as I have always been respectful of those who support this industry by watching and loving these characters we are so dang lucky and blessed to portray and bring to life.
I really enjoyed Sully and fought for his voice and his character. The richness and depth of Sully, whom the fans of the books all know and love, is so multi-layered and interesting.

The fans deserved better than to think the embodiment of this character, me, would just disrespect not only the show, but them. In the end, we’re all fans of these characters and stories, and I’ll always support and defend the truth. ~ Scott
(TODAY.com has reached out to the CW for comment on Patterson's statement but did not immediately hear back.)

title: Harry Styles Explains the Lyrics to His New Song 'Paint By Numbers'
summary: Is Paint By Numbers about Olivia Wilde? Harry Styles explains the lyrics to the song.
story: Harry Styles’ fourth studio album, “Kiss All the Time. Disco, Occasionally,” is officially out in the world, and fans are trying to decode the lyrics to each of the 12 songs.
One of the tracks, “Paint By Numbers,” is a melancholy ballad that finds Styles seemingly reflecting on a romantic relationship and fame.
In an interview with Apple Music’s Zane Lowe, Styles revealed that “Paint By Numbers” was originally the first track on the album.

“I think I was just married to the idea of coming off the last record and coming back and the first thing I say being, ‘Oh, what a gift it is to be noticed but it’s nothing to do with me.’ I just loved that,” he said, referencing one of the song’s lyrics.

In the song, Styles sings he’s “holdin’ the weight of the American children whose hearts you break,” seemingly referring to fans’ disappointment whenever they discover that he’s in a romantic relationship.
The 32-year-old goes on to reference an older woman, singing, “Was it a tragedy when you told her ‘I’m not even 33’?”
Styles notably dated actor Olivia Wilde, who is nine years older than he is. The couple went their separate ways in 2022.

While examining the meaning of the song, Lowe noted that “Paint By Numbers” could have easily been something Styles wrote in an effort to “figure something out, to move on past,” but not necessarily include on the album.
He then asked why Styles chose to include it, even though it “stands alone in the context of the record.”
Styles replied by saying he’s always respected musicians who let their audience into their inner lives.
“Some of my favorite moments for artists I love is when I feel like I’m listening to them discover themselves,” he said.

“I think writing it and keeping it on my hard drive is — I don’t know what feels brave about that in any way,” he continued. “The thing that makes someone an artist is letting them watch you be an ordinary person. It’s not about being, ‘I am this mystic thing.’ We’re all ordinary people and there’s some people who let you watch you be an ordinary person.”
Read the lyrics to “Paint By Numbers,” a song about Styles figuring it all out, below.
Read the lyrics to ‘Paint by Numbers’
Oh, what a gift it is to be noticed
But it’s nothin’ to do with me
You’ve got to wonder if there’s a reason to believe
It’s a lifetime of learnin’ to paint by numbers
And watchin’ the colours run

When they put an image in your head, and now you’re stuck with it
You’re the luckiest, oh, the irony
Holdin’ the weight of the American children whose hearts you break
Was it a tragedy when you told her
“I’m not even thirty-three”?
A little self-compassion and a life within your means
It’s a lifetime of pickin’ from one or the other
Kids with water guns, watch them run
It’s a lifetime of learnin’ to paint by numbers
And watchin’ the colours run

title: Daryl Hannah Breaks Silence on "Love Story" in a Fiery Opinion Piece
summary: Daryl Hannah slams Love Story and her portrayal in it. The show depicts her relationship with John F Kennedy Jr.
story: Daryl Hannah pulls no punches in blasting “Love Story” and how she is portrayed in the FX series that follows the relationship between John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette.
Hannah, who dated JFK Jr. before he married Bessette, blasts the show in a New York Times op-ed published March 6.

“I have generally chosen not to respond to media coverage of me,” she wrote. “I have long believed that engaging with distortion often amplifies it. But a recent tragedy-exploiting television series about John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette features a character using my name and presents her as me. The choice to portray her as irritating, self-absorbed, whiny and inappropriate was no accident.”
Hannah, who is portrayed in “Love Story” by Dree Hemingway, says the version of her depicted onscreen falls well short of who she is.
“The character ‘Daryl Hannah’ portrayed in the series is not even a remotely accurate representation of my life, my conduct or my relationship with John,” she wrote.

“The actions and behaviors attributed to me are untrue. I have never used cocaine in my life or hosted cocaine-fueled parties. I have never pressured anyone into marriage. I have never desecrated any family heirloom or intruded upon anyone’s private memorial. I have never planted any story in the press. I never compared Jacqueline Onassis’ death to a dog’s. It’s appalling to me that I even have to defend myself against a television show. These are not creative embellishments of personality. They are assertions about conduct — and they are false.”
Hannah, 65, said the response she has gotten from the show has been negative.

“When so many people watch a dramatization that uses a real name, real-life consequences follow,” she wrote. “In the weeks since the series aired, I have received many hostile and even threatening messages from viewers who seem to believe the portrayal is factual. When entertainment borrows a real person’s name, it can permanently impact her reputation.”
Hannah wrote that she focuses on “keeping my private life private,” while noting the Kennedys also value that.

“The Kennedy family is also notoriously private, and I have always honored their right to privacy,” she wrote. “Know that most (if not all) of those claiming to have any intimate knowledge of our personal lives are self-serving sensationalists trading in gossip, innuendo and speculation.

“Many people believe what they see on TV and do not distinguish between dramatization and documented fact — and the impact is not abstract. In a digital era, entertainment often becomes collective memory. Real names are not fictional tools. They belong to real lives.”

Hannah and Kennedy dated in the late ‘80s and early ‘90s, even appearing on the cover of People in 1993 before splitting in 1994. She is currently married to Neil Young. She is known for starring in movies such as “Splash,” “Blade Runner,” “Roxanne,” “Wall Street,” “Steel Magnolias,” “A Walk to Remember” and the “Kill Bill” films.
Hemingway said she took the Hannah role seriously and is a big fan of hers, while noting “Love Story” took some liberties.

“I mean, I love her so much,” she told The Hollywood Reporter in February. “It’s an incredible and iconic shoe to fill, but Ryan (Murphy) and Connor Hines, who wrote everything, were so great at characterizing Daryl. The show is definitely told from a fictional aspect because we don’t know what happened (behind closed doors). But it was amazing. She’s amazing.”
Hemingway, who said while she doesn’t know Hannah, her mother, the actor Mariel Hemingway does. Hemingway even tried to contact her.

“I reached out (to Hannah). I have no expectations of her reaching out to me in any way, but I basically wrote her a love note of how much I appreciate her as a woman, as an actress, and what an honor of my life this has been,” she said.

title: Eva Mendes Makes Rare Joint Appearance with Husband Ryan Gosling for Surprise Birthday Serenade
summary: Ryan Gosling pulled off a sweet birthday surprise for Eva Mendes during his latest interview.
story: Ryan Gosling pulled off a sweet birthday surprise for Eva Mendes during his latest interview.
While appearing on "The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon," Gosling casually mentioned that it was his wife's birthday and revealed that she was hanging out backstage.

The actor, who plays a middle school teacher in his new film "Project Hail Mary," then addressed the audience, which was full of teachers, to tell them how much respect Mendes has for their profession.
"You have no idea how much she loves teachers, loves you so much. She has her hall monitor sash still from when she was a kid. She hangs it proudly in the house," he said.
Fallon then interjected with, "She has no idea we’re doing this, by the way."
Gosling continued, asking the audience if they'd sing "Happy Birthday" to Mendes if he got her to come out on stage.
"She's gonna kill you," Fallon teased.
"Now, here’s the thing, you might find me in the Hudson River tomorrow," Gosling joked.
The camera then panned to a staff member backstage who escorted Mendes out of a dressing room and onto the stage.
The crowd cheered as Gosling took Mendes by the hand. It was a rare joint appearance for the couple, who prefer to keep their family life private.

Mendes took the opportunity to address the teachers in the audience, saying, "I love teachers so much, you guys. We owe so much to you guys. You’re so underpaid. Thank you so much. I had so many amazing teachers."
Mendes, who assumed that her appearance wouldn't be included in the episode, then asked Fallon, "You’ll cut all this, right?"
"No!" he replied.
Mendes went on to shower praise on the teachers in the audience.
"I had so many amazing teachers mentor me. And I just want to say thank you to you guys," she said.
Fallon then mentioned Mendes' birthday and said they had "something special" in store for her.
New Jersey's North Bergen High School band then appeared with a flag that read, "Happy Birthday Eva," and played "Happy Birthday" for Mendes, who clapped along enthusiastically.
Mendes, who turned 52 on March 5, turned to thank Fallon, but he gave credit to Gosling for the surprise.
"That's all him!" Fallon said.

title: Harry Styles Says 'American Girls' Was Inspired By Watching His 3 Friends Get Married: 'Quite a Lonely Song'
summary: What is American Girls by Harry Styles about? He says it's about his evolving thoughts on marriage and watching all of his friends get married.
story: Harry Styles has something to say about American girls on his newly released album, “Kiss All The Time. Disco, Occasionally.”
The second track on the record features the British pop star singing about how all of his friends are “in love with American girls.”
Styles gave insight into what prompted him to create the playful song “American Girls” in an interview with Apple Music’s Zane Lowe ahead of the album’s release on March 6.
American girls will be singing along to it in person when Styles kicks off a 30-date residency at New York City’s Madison Square Garden on Aug. 26.
It turns out that Styles’ actual friends in real life are in love with American girls.

“The song to me is like, it’s actually quite a lonely song in a lot of ways,” Styles said. “I watched like my three closest friends get married, and actually seeing them trust in something and risk something to find something truly fulfilling in a way that isn’t as like shiny and on paper as exciting.

“Watching them get married, I was like, ‘I’m single, so I’m having all the fun. And ‘American Girls’ is actually about watching them get married, and there just is a magic when you find the right person that you want to be with. I think watching them do that and seeing that it doesn’t come without any uncertainty, it doesn’t come without any risk.”
Seeing his friends tie the knot prompted Styles, 32, to think about his own life.

“It’s like being truly vulnerable with someone, sharing a life with someone like that,” he said. “Having the time to stop and assess all of it and really look at my life from a bird’s eye view and go like, ‘What do I actually want in my life?’ I have all these things around me all the time. It’s hard to pull those things in without making space for them.”

“So if I stop everything, then I get to decide what I want to bring back in,” he continued. “What are really the things that I want in my life? If you’re touring all the time and you’re doing this all the time, and all these things, there’s no space to really choose.”
Styles said he had “a real honest conversation” with himself about what he wants his life to look like in five years and what changes he needs to make to create that future.

“I want to be fulfilled, and I want to be in great relationships with people, I want to have great friendships with people, I want a family. I want these things,” he said. “It just allowed me to go like, ‘OK, what do I have to do to create space to allow these things to happen? I can’t just expect them to just happen to me.”
One of the world’s most famous bachelors also expanded on what he wants in a partner.

“That’s part of why you choose that person is because they hold you accountable to the person that they know you want to be,” he said. “I think accepting flaws and stuff, yes, having understanding is wonderful, but you know I want to be better than that, so when you challenge me on something, it’s a gift to me actually.”

title: Drew Barrymore Recalls JFK Jr. Asking Her To Pose As Marilyn Monroe for His Magazine
summary: Drew Barrymore recalled the time John F. Kenneedy Jr. called her to ask about posing as Marilyn Monroe for the cover of his George magazine.
story: John F. Kennedy Jr. is having a moment, thanks to the FX series “Love Story,” which chronicles his relationship with Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy. Drew Barrymore, meanwhile, is re-living her own moment with Kennedy.
The actor reflected on the time she posed as Marilyn Monroe on the September 1996 cover of his magazine, George, and how JFK Jr. initially called her to broach the idea.
“I’ll never forget. I was in my home, I was in bed,” she told Valerie Bertinelli and Ross Mathews on the March 5 episode of “The Drew Barrymore Show.”

“So, I get on the phone and I’m like, ‘Hi.’ I mean, we were at the pinnacle of this couple being, I guess, as big as Taylor (Swift) and Travis (Kelce),” Barrymore said about JFK Jr. and Bessette-Kennedy.

“I don’t know how they lived their life. Their existence was not peaceful,” Barrymore later added. “It was paparazzi every single second, everywhere that they went. They couldn’t walk out their own door without it being a red carpet fashion runway. And they were Camelot. They were this sort of royal couple.”
When he called with his vision for the cover, Barrymore had some questions, she recalled.

“He said, ‘I want to do something that’s going be controversial because I’d like you to pose as Marilyn Monroe.' And, of course, I know the famous, ‘Happy birthday, Mr. President,’ very well. We all do,” she said, referring to Monroe's sultry performance for Kennedy's father in 1962, which

“And I said, ‘Oh, OK.’ ... I said, ‘You know, I’d really love some direction from you on what the tone is. Are you trying to say this gets your seal of approval? Are you making fun of it. Are you just secretly, slyly kind of acknowledging it in a very empowered way. How can I best represent this moment for you?'" she recalled.
She said he didn’t want to overthink the matter.

“He just said, ‘I want it to be straightforward. I don’t want it to be buffoon-y. I don’t want it to be over-congratulatory. I want it to be this sort of sensual, straightforward, confident moment,’” Barrymore said.
“And then we went and did the shoot and I heard they were really happy with the pictures and that was great. And then I saw the magazine and I was like, ‘Oh my God. This is so crazy.’”
George was a political and pop culture magazine co-founded by JFK Jr. Issues ran from 1995 until 2001, two years after he and Bessette-Kennedy died in a plane crash near Martha's Vineyard.
“Love Story” continues to be a massive hit, introducing a new generation of fans to the couple's relationship while becoming FX’s most-watched limited series ever on Hulu and Disney+.

title: What Time Is 'Outlander' On? How to Watch the Season 8 Premiere
summary: When does Outlander Season 8 start? How to watch the Season 8 premiere and what to know about the new season including the start time and episode schedule.
story: The final season of "Outlander" has arrived, and its conclusion will be a surprise to all — including its stars.
Sam Heughan and Caitriona Balfe have played star and timeline-crossed lovers Jamie and Claire Fraser since the show premiered on Starz in 2014.
Heughan, during a March 4 visit to TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle, that he isn't sure which of the multiple endings filmed will make the final cut.
“I haven’t seen it. I don’t know what happens,” Heughan said.
He did, however, say the moment that made the audience at the "Outlander" Season 8 premiere "erupt": The return of Brianna, Jamie and Claire's daughter, and her family, who are back from the future.
Read more below to find out when "Outlander" Season 8 premieres on Starz.
When Does 'Outlander' Season 8 Premiere?
"Outlander" Season 8 premieres on Starz on Friday, March 6.
What Time Does 'Outlander' Season 8 Premiere?
"Outlander" Season 8 will premiere on Starz streaming platforms at 12 a.m. ET on Friday, March 6.
It will also be airing at 8:02 p.m. ET on Starz cable channels.
Where Can I Watch 'Outlander' Season 8?
The eighth season of "Outlander" will be available for streaming on the Starz streaming platform, as well as the Starz cable channel.
What Is the Episode Schedule for 'Outlander' Season 8?
The first episode of "Outlander" will be released on March 6 and is titled "Soul of a Rebel".
The whole season will be 10 episodes, with a new episode being released every Friday.

What Will Happen in Season 8 of 'Outlander'?
The final season is expected to bring Claire and Jamie's epic love story to a close. The season will follow the pair as they return to North Carolina, only to find more conflict.

An official description from Starz reads as follows: "As Season Eight begins, Jamie (Sam Heughan) and Claire (Caitríona Balfe) soon find the war has followed them home to Fraser’s Ridge, now a thriving settlement that has grown and flourished in their absence. With new arrivals and changes made during their years away, the Frasers are confronted with the question of what they are willing to sacrifice for the place they call home and, more importantly, what they would sacrifice to stay together. While the Frasers keep a united front against outside intruders, family secrets finally coming to light threaten to tear them apart from the inside. Although they’ve left the war for America’s freedom behind, their fight for Fraser’s Ridge has only just begun.”

title: What Did Carolyn Bessette's Mom Really Say At Her Wedding?
summary: Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy's mom, Ann Messina Freeman, allegedly gave a critical toast at her daughter's wedding to JFK Jr. Here's what to know.
story: Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy and John F. Kennedy Jr.’s wedding was an intimate celebration of the couple’s love.
Despite photographers’ keen desire to get a peek at their big day, the bride and groom managed to pull off their nuptials in private.
Similar to any wedding, the day didn’t exactly go off without a hitch. For instance, the ceremony started two hours late due to a wardrobe malfunction.
“Love Story” also shows another element from the wedding: The alleged toast Bessette’s mother, Ann Messina Freeman, gave during the rehearsal dinner.
What did Messina, played by Constance Zimmer, really say in her toast, and how did she feel about her daughter’s relationship with a Kennedy? Here’s what accounts from the wedding have to say.
Two Books Shed Light on What Happened
One of Kennedy’s close friends, Robert Littell, who was present at the wedding, penned a memoir titled, “The Men We Became: My Friendship with John F. Kennedy, Jr.”
In the book, Littell recalled the experience of attending Kennedy and Bessette’s wedding rehearsal dinner, and mentioned a speech from Bessette’s mother.

“It was boisterous and lighthearted, for the most part, except for a toast made by Carolyn’s mother, Ann Freeman. I don’t remember her exact words, but she implied that she was worried for her daughter, unsure if this union was in her best interest. In hindsight, it’s chilling. At the time, I was surprised by her bluntness and felt bad for John, who was visibly stung by the remarks,” he wrote.

In the book “Once Upon a Time: The Captivating Life of Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy,” Elizabeth Beller references Littell’s memoir and elaborated on why Freeman might have spoken out at the rehearsal dinner.

“Carolyn, after all, had always been a private individual; marrying a public figure would be tantamount to being thrust under a microscope — every aspect of her personal life would be explored and exploited by the media,” she wrote. “Littell described how the crowd, happy and enthusiastic about the union, moved on quickly from the moment, but that John was ‘visibly stung by his mother-in-law’s remarks.’”
Who Was Ann Freeman?
Ann Freeman was the mother of Bessette Kennedy and her twin sisters, Lisa and Lauren Bessette.
Lauren Bessette died in the same plane crash that killed Kennedy and Bessette Kennedy in 1999.
Per a documentary titled, “JFK And Carolyn’s Wedding: The Lost Tapes,” Freeman and her husband divorced when her daughters were very young.
J. Randy Taraborrelli, the author of “The Kennedy Heirs,” appeared in the documentary and offered commentary on how Bessette’s parents’ divorce impacted her.

“I think she was very heavily influenced by the fact that her mom got on with her life, you know, that her mom wasn’t ruined by the divorce was important to her. I think it was a family trait in the sense that you weren’t a shrinking violet and she was very self-confident,” Taraborrelli said.

In “Once Upon a Time: The Captivating Life of Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy,” Beller described how Freeman remarried after her divorce. This is where she got her last name from: her husband, orthopedic surgeon Dr. Richard G. Freeman.

Bessette’s mother, stepfather and two sisters were present at her wedding to Kennedy. After the plane crash in 1999, the Freemans shared a statement with reporters, describing their daughters as “the embodiment of love, accomplishment, and passion for life.”

“John and Carolyn were true soul mates, and we hope to honor them in death in the simple manner in which they chose to live their lives. We take solace in the thoughts that together they will comfort Lauren for eternity,” the statement read, per Beller.
Ann Messina Freeman died in 2007.

title: The True Story Behind Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy's Wedding Dress
summary: This is the true story of Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy's wedding dress, including the process of making it and how much it cost.
story: Carolyn Bessette Kennedy was the picture of elegance at her wedding to John F. Kennedy Jr. in 1996.

Despite their best efforts, paparazzi didn't catch a glimpse of the newlyweds at their nuptials. The couple only allowed one photographer to attend: Denis Reggie, the brother of Sen. Ted Kennedy’s wife.
In a 2021 TODAY segment, Reggie shared several photos from the couple's wedding day, which took place on Cumberland Island, a small island off the coast of Georgia.
The bride and groom said "I do" at the First African Baptist Church in front of around 40 guests.

For the special occasion, Bessette Kennedy sported a stunning gown from Narciso Rodriguez, whom she befriended while they both worked at Calvin Klein. Today, Rodriguez is a hit designer, but back then, his career was still budding.
Below, we're taking a look back at the story behind the bride's beautiful gown.
Rodriguez Gave Bessette Kennedy the Gown as a Gift

Per Elizabeth Beller’s book, “Once Upon a Time: The Captivating Life of Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy,” the estimated cost of Bessette's wedding gown was around $40,000. As it turns out, the designer gave it to her "as a gift."
After asking Rodriguez to design her wedding gown, Bessette flew to Paris several times for three-hour fittings "to ensure the sublime, pearl-colored silk crepe dress fit like a glove."
In her book, Beller describes how these trips to France helped create a "diversion" for wedding planning.

"The papers spotted her dining in Paris with Narciso during one of their trips and speculated that he was a new mystery man, and that Carolyn and John had split, claiming she turned up in a 'sighting last week in Paris at a Chinese eatery on Rue de la Roche,'" the book reads.
The Bride Opted for a Sleek and Simple Gown
Rodriguez designed a sleek slip dress with a cowl neckline and an open back. The bride accessorized the look with tulle gloves and a matching veil.
In “JFK And Carolyn’s Wedding: The Lost Tapes,” The Knot editor Esther Lee described how the gown’s design was unique for the time.

“His dress was iconic and it was brilliant and it was beautiful. But at the time it was completely different from any other wedding dress during the ‘90s that we had seen. During that decade, we saw a lot of ball gowns and princess-y type of pieces. And yet here’s Carolyn Bessette. She’s essentially becoming America’s princess and she doesn’t choose anything out of a fairy tale movie. Instead, she chose something that was elegant and structured,” Lee said.
Bessette Put Her Makeup On Before the Gown, Leading to a Delay

In her book, Beller describes how the gown's design led to a delay in the wedding ceremony. The nuptials were supposed to begin at 5 p.m. but ended up starting two hours late after a few wardrobe mishaps.

“John had misplaced his shirt, and Carolyn tried to put her dress on in the car on the way to the chapel. She forgot that she needed to put the dress on before attending to her hair and makeup, which meant redoing her hair and makeup after she had pulled the form-fitting work of art over hear head,” the book reads.
The documentary “JFK And Carolyn’s Wedding: The Lost Tapes” describes how Bessette's friends ended up draping a silk scarf over her head and across her shoulders to slide the dress down her body.
Nonetheless, it set the bride back a few hours.
When all was said and done, Bessette Kennedy paired the gown with a simple beauty look. Per Beller, she wore her hair in a "low, twisted bun at her neck" with "barely there makeup."
In the book, Beller described Rodgriguez's reaction to seeing the bride all done up on her wedding day.
"Narciso maybe put it best: 'I'll never see anything more beautiful than that,'" the book reads.

title: Britney Spears Arrested in California, Set to Appear in Court in May
summary: Britney Spears was arrested by the California Highway Patrol, according to the Ventura County Sheriff's Office.
story: Britney Spears was arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence on March 4 in California, according to the Ventura County Sheriff's Office.
The arrest was made by the California Highway Patrol at about 9:30 p.m. local time in Ventura. The pop star's car was towed.
Spears, 44, was released shortly after 6 a.m. on March 5, and is due to appear in court on May 4, according to the sheriff's office.
A CHP spokesperson said a report was made around 8:48 p.m. after a driver in a black BMW 430i was driving erratically and at high speed on the southbound 101 Freeway.

Spears, who was the only person in the vehicle, was pulled over and showed signs of impairment. She was given field sobriety tests and arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence of a combination of drugs and alcohol, the spokesperson added.
The incident remains under investigation and chemical tests are pending, per the spokesperson.

“This was an unfortunate incident that is completely inexcusable,” a representative for Spears said in a statement to TODAY.com. “Britney is going to take the right steps and comply with the law and hopefully this can be the first step in long overdue change that needs to occur in Britney’s life. Hopefully, she can get the help and support she needs during this difficult time.
“Her boys are going to be spending time with her. Her loved ones are going to come up with an overdue needed plan to set her up for success for well being,” the statement continued.

The arrest comes less than a month after the "Toxic" singer sold the rights to her music catalog to publisher Primary Wave for approximately $200 million, a source with knowledge of the deal told NBC News.
Spears spent 13 years under a conservatorship that legally prevented her from making her own personal and financial  decisions before a Los Angeles judge terminated the arrangement in 2021.
She wrote about her life under the conservatorship in her 2023 memoir, “The Woman in Me."

“I had been so infantilized that I was losing pieces of what made me feel like myself,” Spears wrote. “The conservatorship stripped me of my womanhood, made me into a child. I became more of an entity than a person onstage. I had always felt music in my bones and my blood; they stole that from me.”

Kevin Federline, her ex-husband and the father of her two sons, Sean Preston, 20, and Jayden James, 19, wrote in his memoir released last October, "You Thought You Knew," that he was concerned about Spears.
“The truth is, this situation with Britney feels like it’s racing toward something irreversible,” he added.

“It’s become impossible to pretend everything’s OK,” he continued. “From where I sit, the clock is ticking, and we’re getting close to the 11th hour. Something bad is going to happen if things don’t change, and my biggest fear is that our sons will be left holding the pieces.”
In October 2025, a representative for Spears responded to Federline's book.

“With news from Kevin’s book breaking, once again he and others are profiting off her and sadly it comes after child support has ended with Kevin,” the rep said in a statement to NBC News. “All she cares about are her kids, Sean Preston and Jayden James and their well-being during this sensationalism. She detailed her journey in her memoir.”

In the years since being freed from her conservatorship, she married — and later divorced — Sam Asghari. She has continued to draw attention online for her Instagram posts, which often feature her dancing in front of the camera or sharing stock images with cryptic captions.

title: 'Vladimir' Ending: What Really Happens to Vlad, John and Rachel Weisz's Character?
summary: What happens at the end of Vladimir? Do John and Vladimir survive? What happens to Rachel Weisz's Character?
story: Warning: Spoilers ahead for the finale of “Vladimir.”
When “Vladimir” opens, Rachel Weisz’s unnamed protagonist tells the camera that it has come to her attention that she “may never again have power over another human being.”

The seemingly sweet middle-aged university professor informs viewers that her students find her “out of touch,” her daughter “useless” and “as an older woman” she may never arouse a man ever again and has “lost the ability to captivate.”
She says all this as she walks by Leo Woodall’s Vladimir, a young and handsome new assistant professor — who also happens to be unconscious and chained to a chair.

As the season goes on, viewers discover that Weisz’s character’s husband, John (John Slattery), is accused of having sexual relationships with students. This, she knew about since they have an open marriage.
So when she attempts to get close to Vlad, a father of one and married to Cynthia, who is also teaching at the same university, it doesn’t really faze John.
What happens at the end of “Vladimir” and why is he tied up in her cabin? Read on to find out.
What Happens at the Cabin?
Vlad and Weisz's character have lunch and over wine they discuss their books. She decides to take him to her cabin so they can do some writing and then things take a turn.

She keeps the booze flowing and at one point even puts something in his drink that makes him black out. This is when she ties him up so that he doesn't topple over. While he's unconscious, she uses his cell to text Cynthia and call her out about having an affair with John. She then drops his phone in water to hide her evidence.

When he wakes up, he's confused and screaming. She lies to him and tells him that they got really drunk and he told her he wanted to be “dominated.” She tells him that he got upset after he found out that Cynthia and John were together.

He goes to sleep in the guest room and the next morning she thinks he's gone but he's not. He decides to stay at the cabin, upset that Cynthia gets to do whatever she wants and he doesn't. He also calls out Weisz's character for texting Cynthia. He knows he didn't do it because he saw the exchange on his laptop that's connected to his phone.

They spend the day together, writing their books and having dinner. When they finish eating and she’s cleaning up, Vlad comes up behind her and tells her, “I need to tell you something. I did something bad. I didn’t hand in my paper, professor. Am I in trouble?”
Confused, she excuses herself and goes outside. Vlad follows and apologizes, asking her if that wasn't what they were going to do last night.
She says she doesn't want to play out a fantasy of her being "some pervy older woman." Vlad, however, says he just wants to do what she wants.
“Oh, Vlad, did I make it all up?” she asks about the possible sexual tension between them. “Oh, I’m going to go get ready for bed.”
Vlad replies, “Should I join you?”
He follows her into her bedroom and they start making out. Vlad tells her everything he has been wanting to do to her and then proceeds to do it.
After their moment together, John walks in and Weisz's character and Vlad confront him about being with Cynthia.
John and Cynthia Are Not Together
John tells them that he and Cynthia have not been hooking up.
